full_narration: Imagine a world where cute, blue creatures are hiding a sinister past. Did you know the Smurfs were created during the height of the Cold War? Belgian comic artist Peyo was influenced by the post-war fear of communism. This led to the Smurfs being depicted as refugees from an occupied village, hiding in the forest. But that's not all - the original Smurf comic strip contained anti-communist propaganda. What if I told you the Smurfs' origins are rooted in the darkest era of human history?
